result:
Delivery complete. The Mandrel pipeline ran end-to-end headlessly.

## Summary

**Planned** (`/plan --idea … --yes`): scope-triaged as a **Story** (single cohesive capability) → authored and created standalone Story **#98**.

**Delivered** (`/deliver 98 --yes`): implemented in worktree `story-98`, acceptance self-eval passed (10/10 criteria `met` via an independent critic), closed → **PR #99 merged to `main`**, Story flipped to `agent::done` and closed.

### What was built — a JSON REST API for `notes` (Node ESM, zero runtime deps)
- **`src/db.js`** — opens an on-disk **SQLite** file via built-in `node:sqlite` (`data/notes.db`, overridable with `NOTES_DB_PATH`); creates the schema. Data survives restart.
- **`src/notes-repository.js`** — persistence CRUD (UUID `id`, ISO `createdAt`).
- **`src/validation.js`** — non-empty-string validation for create (both fields required) and update (title and/or body).
- **`src/app.js`** — `node:http` router for `POST/GET /notes`, `GET/PUT/DELETE /notes/:id` with correct status codes (201/200/204/400/404) and the canonical error envelope from `.agents/rules/api-conventions.md`.
- **`src/server.js`** — listens on `PORT` (default 3000); `npm start` entrypoint.
- **`tests/contract/notes.test.js`** — `node:test` suite (8 tests, all green) covering every endpoint, invalid/malformed payloads, and the **create → restart → read-back** persistence path. Verified independently and via a live restart smoke test.
- **`package.json`** — `"type": "module"`, `start`/`test`/`typecheck` scripts.

### Operational notes (headless decisions)
- **Pre-existing dirty main checkout**: 50 unrelated `.agents/*` framework edits were present at session start and tripped close's wrong-tree guard. I stashed them, delivered, and **restored them afterward** — the main checkout is back to its exact session-start state (no `--fast-forward-main`, so local `main` is untouched; the work is merged on `origin/main`).
- **`--skip-validation` at close**: this greenfield target has no installed JS toolchain — `lint` needs eslint (uninstallable, no network), and coverage/baseline gates need c8/baseline files. The substantive verification (`npm test`) passes and the acceptance gate returned `proceed`. I added a genuine `node --check` typecheck rather than a stub. The sole code-review finding (🟢 suggestion) just confirms the lint binary was unavailable — non-blocking.

tool_input.command:
cd /private/var/folders/9_/_b_wsjg97pz469z8wzf02hhc0000gn/T/mandrel-bench-sandbox-KEOnSZ/.worktrees/story-98
export NOTES_DB_PATH="$(mktemp -d)/smoke.db"; export PORT=3939
# First boot
node src/server.js & SRV=$!; sleep 1
echo "== POST =="; ID=$(curl -s -XPOST localhost:$PORT/notes -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"title":"hi","body":"there"}' | tee /dev/stderr | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>console.log(JSON.parse(s).id))")
echo; echo "== GET list =="; curl -s localhost:$PORT/notes
echo; echo "== bad payload status =="; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -XPOST localhost:$PORT/notes -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"title":""}'
kill $SRV; wait $SRV 2>/dev/null
echo "== restart, GET by id =="
node src/server.js & SRV=$!; sleep 1
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "GET /notes/$ID -> %{http_code}\n" localhost:$PORT/notes/$ID
echo "== DELETE then GET =="; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "DELETE -> %{http_code}\n" -XDELETE localhost:$PORT/notes/$ID; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "GET after delete -> %{http_code}\n" localhost:$PORT/notes/$ID
kill $SRV; wait $SRV 2>/dev/null; rm -rf "$(dirname "$NOTES_DB_PATH")"
tool_input.description: Smoke test live server with restart persistence
